question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where getPreviousHash() returns a string that is byte-for-byte equal to the expected hash but uses a different character encoding or Unicode normalization form, exploiting the contentEquals() comparison in verifyHashChain() to either pass or fail verification in an attacker-controlled manner, allowing selective acceptance of forged Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where streamFile.copy().clear() produces a copy with a null or empty hash value, causing lastStreamFile to be updated with a cleared hash, so that the next call to verify() retrieves null as expectedPrevHash, which then passes the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() check in verifyHashChain() and skips chain verification for the subsequent file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadNextBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated SignatureVerificationException throws from nodeSignatureVerifier.verify() by injecting malformed signature files, causing downloadNextBatch() to log warnings and silently skip entire batches of legitimate AccountBalanceFiles, creating persistent gaps in the mirror node's Hashgraph balance history without any alerting mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifySignatures() iterates over signatures in an arbitrary order (due to STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR using Ordering.arbitrary()), by timing the injection of a forged signature file such that it is processed first and its hash is used as the reference for consensus, before legitimate signatures are checked?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the listLimit being set to Long.MAX_VALUE when downloaderProperties.isEnabled() is true, by flooding the stream file provider with an enormous number of crafted signature file entries, causing the Flux to process billions of entries and exhaust memory or processing capacity, preventing legitimate balance files from being downloaded and corrupting Hashgraph history continuity?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getStartAfterFilename()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the StreamFilename.from() parsing of the last stream file's name to produce a StreamFilename that sorts lexicographically before all existing files, causing the downloader to re-download and re-process all historical AccountBalanceFiles from the beginning, potentially overwriting correct Hashgraph history with tampered versions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where streamFileProvider.get(dataFilename).block() returns null for all CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures, causing Objects.requireNonNull() to throw NullPointerException which is caught by the generic Exception handler, logging an error and moving to the next node, until all nodes are exhausted and verifySignatures() returns false, permanently skipping a legitimate balance file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a balance file with a consensusStart value of Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the latency calculation (streamFile.getConsensusStart() - last.getConsensusStart()) to overflow and record a wildly incorrect metric, while also poisoning the lastStreamFile cache with a file whose consensusStart is used as the anchor for all future hash chain and ordering validations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: shouldDownload()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when downloaderProperties.isEnabled() is false, shouldDownload() falls through to check accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest(), by deleting or corrupting the latest balance file record in the repository, causing shouldDownload() to return true and trigger a download cycle that fetches attacker-controlled balance files as the first entry in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Scheduled annotation's fixedDelayString using the balanceDownloaderProperties.getFrequency() value by manipulating the frequency to an extremely small value (e.g., 1ms), causing download() to be called in a tight loop that overwhelms the signature verification pipeline and creates race conditions in the lastStreamFile AtomicReference updates, corrupting hash chain state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance data file that passes signature verification (fileHash and metadataHash match the signatures) but contains manipulated account balance entries, exploiting the fact that verify() only checks fileHash and metadataHash but does not validate the integrity of individual AccountBalance records within the file against any Hashgraph consensus record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that verifyHashChain() uses contentEquals() instead of a constant-time comparison, enabling a timing side-channel attack where the attacker can iteratively determine the expected previous hash byte-by-byte by measuring response times, and then forge a balance file with the correct previousHash to pass chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifySignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a balance file where streamFileReader.read(streamFileData) throws a non-FileOperationException, non-HashMismatchException, non-TransientProviderException (e.g., OutOfMemoryError or StackOverflowError), causing the generic Exception catch block to log an error and try the next node, exhausting all CONSENSUS_REACHED nodes and returning false, permanently skipping a legitimate balance file from Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/AccountBalancesDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadAndParseSigFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that streamFileSignature.setNode(node) and streamFileSignature.setStreamType(streamType) are called after signatureFileReader.read() in downloadAndParseSigFiles(), by crafting a signature file that causes read() to return a StreamFileSignature with a pre-set node pointing to a high-stake legitimate node, allowing the attacker's forged signature to be attributed to a trusted node?
